programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cooking
programs.description: In this camp, you will learn the art of cooking. On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cooking
programs.description: In this camp, you will learn the art of cooking. On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cooking
programs.description: In this camp, you will learn the art of cooking. On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.description: Get ready to create. Using a variety of arts and crafts materials, skills and your imagination, you will make unique craft projects. programs.name: CampTO Plus: Creative Writing
programs.description: This camp is for those who have a story to tell. Come share your stories with us as you learn the basics of writing with a focus on thought organization, plot and character development. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your creative writing skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Advanced (On the Road)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is great way to take your cycling to the next level. Learn how to ride safely and skillfully on residential streets, through guided practice and instruction. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cycling skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cheerleading
programs.description: Fun is the order of the day in this high energy camp. From learning dances and acrobatic movements to getting loud with shouts and chants, you will gain basic cheerleading skills that you can show off at an end-of-session performance.  In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your skills in cheerleading, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Circus
programs.description: Have you ever wanted to join the circus? If so, we have the next best thing. In this camp, you will learn magic, juggling, acrobatics, public speaking and performance skills—everything to get you ready for your debut performance under the “big top.”In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your circus skills , you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Crafts
programs.description: Get ready to create. Using a variety of arts and crafts materials, skills and your imagination, you will make unique craft projects. All creations will be taken home at the end of the week.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on crafts, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Creative Writing

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Advanced (On the Road)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is great way to take your cycling to the next level. Learn how to ride safely and skillfully on residential streets, through guided practice and instruction. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cycling skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Beginner
programs.description: For brand-new and beginner riders this camp is great way to learn to bicycle. programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cheerleading
programs.description: Fun is the order of the day in this high energy camp. From learning dances and acrobatic movements to getting loud with shouts and chants, you will gain basic cheerleading skills that you can show off at an end-of-session performance.  In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your skills in cheerleading, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Circus
programs.description: Have you ever wanted to join the circus? If so, we have the next best thing. In this camp, you will learn magic, juggling, acrobatics, public speaking and performance skills—everything to get you ready for your debut performance under the “big top.”In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your circus skills , you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cooking
programs.description: In this camp, you will learn the art of cooking. On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Advanced (On the Road)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is great way to take your cycling to the next level. Learn how to ride safely and skillfully on residential streets, through guided practice and instruction. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cycling skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Advanced (On the Road)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is great way to take your cycling to the next level. Learn how to ride safely and skillfully on residential streets, through guided practice and instruction. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cycling skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

Through skill introduction, drills and games, you will practice how to balance, pedal, steer and stop all while remaining on-site.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cyclying skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet, and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cooking
programs.description: In this camp, you will learn the art of cooking. On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Advanced (On the Road)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is great way to take your cycling to the next level. Learn how to ride safely and skillfully on residential streets, through guided practice and instruction. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cycling skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Beginner
programs.description: For brand-new and beginner riders this camp is great way to learn to bicycle. Through skill introduction, drills and games, you will practice how to balance, pedal, steer and stop all while remaining on-site.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cyclying  skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet, and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Crafts
programs.description: Get ready to create. Using a variety of arts and crafts materials, skills and your imagination, you will make unique craft projects. All creations will be taken home at the end of the week.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on crafts, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Creative Writing
programs.description: This camp is for those who have a story to tell. Come share your stories with us as you learn the basics of writing with a focus on thought organization, plot and character development. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your creative writing skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Advanced (On the Road)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is great way to take your cycling to the next level. Learn how to ride safely and skillfully on residential streets, through guided practice and instruction. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cycling skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cooking
programs.description: In this camp, you will learn the art of cooking. On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Crafts
programs.description: Get ready to create. Using a variety of arts and crafts materials, skills and your imagination, you will make unique craft projects. All creations will be taken home at the end of the week.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on crafts, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Creative Writing
programs.description: This camp is for those who have a story to tell. Come share your stories with us as you learn the basics of writing with a focus on thought organization, plot and character development. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your creative writing skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cycling - Advanced (On the Road)
programs.description: If you already know how to ride a bike, this camp is great way to take your cycling to the next level. Learn how to ride safely and skillfully on residential streets, through guided practice and instruction. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cycling skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Equipment: Campers must bring their own bicycle helmet and a working bicycle with a bell and lock.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.
